verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a canonical ER-resident, single-pass transmembrane protein. Although it translocates to ER-PM junctional zones upon calcium depletion to activate ORAI1 channels, its TM domain remains embedded in the ER membrane, not in the PM. The protein does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane by its own mechanism; instead, it mediates SOCE signaling from the ER side of the junction. No documented ectodomain shedding, PM insertion, or stable PM-anchoring complex formation occurs.
